title: Use substring_index and rank() in a sql cause wrong result
description:
See a sql with group by/order by/rank: `name2` returns a wrong value '15';

In addition, when test with 8.0.16, there is no such problem. 

mysql> select
    -> group_concat(name order by value2) as name1,
    -> substring_index(group_concat(name order by value2),',',1) as name2,
    -> rank() over (order by sum(value1) desc, max(value2) asc ) as `rank`
    -> from tt2  group by id1,id2 order by sum(value1) desc, max(value2) asc;
+-------+-------+------+
| name1 | name2 | rank |
+-------+-------+------+
| 24,14 | 15    |    1 |
| 23,13 | 15    |    2 |
| 22,12 | 15    |    3 |
| 21,11 | 15    |    4 |
| 10,20 | 15    |    5 |
| 19    | 15    |    6 |
| 18    | 15    |    7 |
| 17    | 15    |    8 |
| 16    | 15    |    9 |
| 15    | 15    |   10 |
+-------+-------+------+
How to repeat:
```
create table tt2 (id1 int, id2 int, value1 int, value2 int, name varchar(32));
insert into tt2 values
(30,00, 10, 10, '10'),
(60,30, 13, 97, '11'),
(90,60, 16, 94, '12'),
(20,90, 19, 91, '13'),
(50,20, 22, 88, '14'),
(80,50, 25, 85, '15'),
(10,80, 28, 82, '16'),
(40,10, 31, 79, '17'),
(70,40, 34, 76, '18'),
(00,70, 37, 73, '19'),
(30,00, 40, 70, '20'),
(60,30, 43, 67, '21'),
(90,60, 46, 64, '22'),
(20,90, 49, 61, '23'),
(50,20, 52, 58, '24');

select id1,id2, sum(value1), max(value2), substring_index(group_concat(name order by value2),',',1), rank() over (order by sum(value1) desc, max(value2) asc ) as `rank` from tt2  group by id1,id2 order by sum(value1) desc, max(value2) asc;

```
Suggested fix:
Is this a bug?
Or whether there is any rule when using rank() function.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE tt3 (id1 INTEGER NOT NULL, id2 INTEGER NOT NULL, value1 INTEGER CHECK (value1 >= 0), value2 INTEGER DEFAULT 0, name TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (id1, id2));
CREATE TABLE tt4 (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, description TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO tt4 (description) VALUES (NULL), ('Test entry 1'), ('Test entry 2');

SELECT id1, id2, SUM(value1) AS total_value, MAX(value2) AS max_value, SUBSTR(GROUP_CONCAT(name ORDER BY value2), 1, INSTR(GROUP_CONCAT(name ORDER BY value2), ',') - 1) AS first_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT SUM(value1) AS total_value, MAX(value2) AS max_value FROM tt3 GROUP BY id1, id2) AS ranked WHERE ranked.total_value > (SELECT SUM(value1) FROM tt3 WHERE id1 = tt3.id1 AND id2 = tt3.id2) OR (ranked.total_value = (SELECT SUM(value1) FROM tt3 WHERE id1 = tt3.id1 AND id2 = tt3.id2) AND ranked.max_value > (SELECT MAX(value2) FROM tt3 WHERE id1 = tt3.id1 AND id2 = tt3.id2))) + 1 AS rank FROM tt3 GROUP BY id1, id2 ORDER BY total_value DESC, max_value ASC;
key_bug_features: Using substring_index with rank() causes incorrect results
